category_title: Elementary students with ADHD (Combined Type) exhibiting disruptive and inattentive behaviors
minimal_context: Alex is working on a creative writing assignment at his desk during an independent work period in the classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our independent creative writing period. Please stay at your desks and work on your stories.

STUDENT: (Fidgets in his chair and quickly scribbles on the paper) I'm done. I skipped some words and made mistakes, but I'm finished.

TUTOR: Please look over your paper and keep trying.

STUDENT: (Stands up beside his desk) I don't want to look it over. Hey guys, look at me!

TUTOR: Alex, you need to be quiet and do your writing.

STUDENT: (Leaves his seat and walks over to distract his peers, making a comical face) Watch this! I just didn't know how to have fun doing this boring writing!

options.A: Engage the student in alternative creative activities during the independent work period, such as drawing, painting, water coloring, and pottery making.
options.B: Ask open-ended questions during the independent creative writing period so that the student can write about topics he already knows.
options.C: Encourage the student to change his self-label from being a "bad kid" to being a good kid who "happens to have some difficulty with a few specific behaviors."
options.D: Give the student the opportunity to voice his opinions during the independent work period and ensure they are valued through dialogic pedagogy.
